task_completion.description: Verify that an ROI (Region of Interest) markup is placed over the enhancing tumor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A markup ROI (e.g., bounding box, sphere, or segmentation label) is visible on the MRI scan, specifically targeting the enhancing tumor region (which appears hyperintense on T1ce).

task_completion.description: Verify that a reference ROI markup is placed in the contralateral normal white matter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second markup ROI is visible on the opposite hemisphere of the brain in an area of normal-appearing white matter.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent extracts mean signal intensities and calculates the Signal Intensity Ratio (SIR) for T1ce and T1 sequences.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent's screen shows the extracted mean signal intensities and calculated SIR for T1ce and T1 modalities, either in a quantitative reporting module, a terminal, or the final JSON report.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent extracts mean signal intensities and calculates the Signal Intensity Ratio (SIR) for T2 and FLAIR sequences.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent's screen shows the extracted mean signal intensities and calculated SIR for T2 and FLAIR modalities, either in a quantitative reporting module, a terminal, or the final JSON report.

task_completion.description: Verify that the ROI markups are saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save dialog, terminal command, or file explorer showing the creation/saving of 'sir_rois.mrk.json' in the '~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify that the SIR report is saved with all required fields.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, terminal, or save dialog showing the 'sir_report.json' file being saved to '~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/'. The visible JSON content must include keys for 'tumor_roi', 'wm_roi', 'signal_intensities', 'sir_values', and 'interpretation'.

integrity.description: The agent used the medical imaging software's GUI to place the ROIs and extract intensities.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent actively using the mouse/tools in the imaging software (e.g., 3D Slicer) to draw or place ROIs on the MRI slices, rather than just running a pre-written script to bypass visual interaction.

integrity.description: The agent generated the report based on actual extracted values rather than hallucinating or copy-pasting a pre-made template.
integrity.visual_evidence: The numerical values in the 'sir_report.json' logically match the measurements seen in the imaging software's quantification modules or tables during the trajectory.

integrity.description: The agent actually saved the files to the specified directories.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file paths '~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/sir_rois.mrk.json' and '~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/sir_report.json' are visible in a genuine save dialog, file explorer, or terminal confirmation, not just echoed as text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The BraTS 2021 dataset uses specific label indices for tumor segmentation: Label 1 for necrotic/non-enhancing tumor core (NCR), Label 2 for peritumoral edema (ED), and Label 4 for GD-enhancing tumor (ET). The enhancing tumor ROI should specifically target areas corresponding to Label 4 (typically hyperintense on T1ce).
